Telehealth

Best HIPAA-compliant telehealth platforms for therapists

Last updated: January 15, 2025 • 5 min read

# Best HIPAA-compliant telehealth platforms for therapists You’ve got a full caseload and a 15-minute gap between sessions. Client three can’t find the Zoom link, client four texts to say the last session never showed up in your notes, and you still need to sign a BAA for the video tool you grabbed in March 2020. If that feels familiar, this post is for you. I’ll walk you through the two HIPAA telehealth platforms therapists actually use—SimplePractice and Doxy.me—so you can pick one and get back to doing therapy. > Quick verdict: Doxy.me gives you free, HIPAA-compliant video in five minutes; SimplePractice bundles telehealth with a full EHR if you want scheduling, notes, and billing in one place. Try DOXY.ME free → | Try SIMPLEPRACTICE free → ## SimplePractice ### Pricing - **US**: $39 Starter, $59 Essential, $79 Plus (telehealth included) - **UK**: £29 Starter, £45 Essential, £60 Plus - **Australia**: AUD 55 Starter, AUD 85 Essential, AUD 115 Plus - **Canada**: CAD 49 Starter, CAD 75 Essential, CAD 99 Plus 30-day free trial, no card required. ### Core features - Unlimited video sessions on every paid plan - Group telehealth up to 15 participants - Screen-sharing and file transfer during calls - Session notes auto-save inside the chart - Client self-scheduling with automated reminders - Mobile app for clients (iOS/Android) ### Pros - Fully integrated EHR—no third-party syncing - Auto-generated BAA signed on every plan - GDPR, PIPEDA, and APP compliance baked in - SOC 2 Type II data centers with AES-256 encryption - 2FA and account audit logs ### Cons - Higher price if you only need video - Free tier limited to 15-min sessions - No white-label option even on Plus ### Best for Therapists who want one login for notes, scheduling, billing, and secure video—especially if you bill insurance and need integrated claim filing. ## Doxy.me ### Pricing - **US**: Free (Clinic), $35 Pro, $50 Clinic + - **UK**: Free, £25 Pro, £38 Clinic + - **Australia**: Free, AUD 45 Pro, AUD 65 Clinic + - **Canada**: Free, CAD 40 Pro, CAD 60 Clinic + Free tier is perpetual; upgrade any time. ### Core features - Browser-based—no downloads for you or clients - Customizable waiting room with music - File transfer and screen-share on Pro+ - White-label room URL on Clinic+ - Group sessions on Clinic+ only - HIPAA-compliant free tier with signed BAA ### Pros - Zero cost to start - Clients click a link—no app install - AES-256 encryption and SOC 2 Type II - GDPR, PIPEDA, and APP documentation available - 5-minute setup; share room URL instantly ### Cons - No EHR or scheduling tools - Group sessions locked behind highest tier - Phone support only on paid plans - Occasional echo complaints on older browsers ### Best for Solo clinicians who already have a calendar and notes system and just need bullet-proof, low-friction video. ## Head-to-head comparison | Feature | SimplePractice | Doxy.me | |---------|----------------|---------| | Lowest HIPAA tier | $39/mo Starter | Free | | Integrated EHR | Yes | No | | Group sessions | Up to 15 on all paid | Up to 12 on Clinic+ | | Client downloads | Mobile app optional | None | | White-label room | No | Yes on Pro+ | | Phone support | US M-F 6 am-5 pm PT | Paid tiers only | | Setup time | <1 hour | 5 minutes | | BAA provided | Auto-signed | Auto-signed on free+ | ## Who should choose which Pick **Doxy.me** if you: - Already use a separate EHR or paper notes - Need to keep overhead low (free is hard to beat) - Want clients to join with zero friction Pick **SimplePractice** if you: - Want scheduling, notes, billing, and video under one roof - Bill insurance and need auto-generated CMS-1500s - Prefer mobile apps for both you and clients ## FAQ ### Is free Doxy.me really HIPAA compliant? Yes. Doxy.me signs a BAA and uses AES-256 encryption even on the free tier. ### Can I use SimplePractice telehealth in the UK? Yes. SimplePractice hosts UK accounts in EU data centers and supplies GDPR documentation. ### Which platform is cheaper for solo practice? Doxy.me free tier costs nothing. SimplePractice Starter starts at $39/£29 per month but includes a full EHR. ### Do clients need to install software? No. Both platforms run in any modern browser on phones, tablets, or computers. ### What telehealth consent form do I need? Both vendors supply templates that reference HIPAA, GDPR, PIPEDA, or APP requirements depending on your country. ## Conclusion If you just need secure video today, grab Doxy.me’s free room and send the link. If you’re tired of juggling three apps to run your practice, SimplePractice bundles HIPAA telehealth with scheduling, notes, and billing—worth the extra monthly cost. Start a 30-day free trial of SimplePractice or create your free Doxy.me room now: Try SIMPLEPRACTICE free → | Try DOXY.ME free →
